what_it_does: This file is a ui-contract resource entry for 'extensions'. It documents the native Claude Code surface for skills, slash commands, plugins, marketplaces, install/enable flows, and the /reload-plugins lifecycle, and it defines the data-model schemas and planned operations a UI authoring/installer tool would render. It repeatedly states that the company exposes no actual extension-management API, so every operation is marked planned and the 'bridge gap' is named explicitly.
observations: A refocus note at the top says previously cited company wrapper endpoints were removed; the entry now treats only native Claude Code surfaces as real.
Identity section defines extension IDs by invocation name and filesystem location, with no skill:// or plugin:// fabric scheme and no company endpoint.
Provides JSON schemas for SKILL.md YAML frontmatter and .claude-plugin/plugin.json manifest.
Lists three operations (extensions.list, extensions.get, extensions.act), all outbound and all status: planned, with no company face.
extensions.act request schema enumerates acts like create-skill, install-plugin, reload-plugins, etc., even though the file says none are exposed today.

Interaction semantics section records native rules: skill content stays in context, plugin layout is strict, versioning by version field or git SHA, workspace trust requirement, additional dirs exception, default-settings plugins.
Defines three resource-level errors: extensions.name-collision, extensions.untrusted, and extensions.not-exposed.
Links section says there are no address-typed fields; skill/plugin references are filesystem paths and identifiers, not fabric addresses.
standouts: The entire resource is deliberately framed as planned/unimplemented, with an explicit 501-style error (extensions.not-exposed) instead of pretending a company API exists.
It supplies concrete JSON schemas for skill frontmatter, plugin manifest, and the act request shape.
Workspace trust is treated as a first-class contract concern with its own error code and UI guidance.
It documents live-change-detection and /reload-plugins semantics as the native reload mechanism.
conventions: Follows the contract-entry frontmatter pattern (type, resource, summary, schemes, status, relates-to).
Uses contract:schema, contract:op, and contract:error fenced blocks consistently.
Labels every operation and binding as planned/unverified, matching the honest 'no company face' stance.
Breaks from an earlier version of the same entry by stripping out company wrapper endpoints and refocusing on native data models.
concerns: The file is almost entirely aspirational: every op is planned and every verification is unverified, so it reads more like a design brief than a binding contract.
The extensions.act request schema could be mistaken for an actual API surface despite the surrounding warnings.
Some sentences are dense with parentheticals and inline URLs, reducing scannability.
No concrete verification evidence or acceptance criteria is provided for any operation.
notes: This is an honest placeholder contract: it names the native Claude Code extension surface and admits the company has no bridge for authoring, installing, or enabling extensions yet.
questions: What exact company system previously owned the removed wrapper endpoints, and why were they removed?
What is the 'supervisor's spawn/inject + R1-prime profile' mechanism now used to drive a real session?
What is a 'composition skill' in the substrate brain and how does it differ from a Claude Code SKILL.md skill?
Are there owners or timelines for moving the planned operations out of planned status?
How would the proposed UI editor/installer actually interact with filesystem paths and the workspace-trust dialog?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches — the stated purpose is to contract the native data model a UI renders while naming the bridge gap, and the file does exactly that, repeatedly clarifying it is not a functional company API.
apparent_themes: Security and trust as a UI concern
intention_signals: It tries to stop UI builders from assuming a company-managed extension API exists, while still giving them the schemas and operation names they would need if such a bridge were ever built, all anchored to real Claude Code native behavior.
novelty: The explicit 'not-exposed' 501 error and repeated 'no company face' framing make this more of a negative contract or anti-API declaration than a typical service definition.
contribution: Keep the precise JSON schemas for skill frontmatter, plugin manifest, and act requests; the catalog of native paths and reload semantics; the workspace-trust and precedence rules; and the explicit status labels that distinguish company capabilities from native-only capabilities.
summary_for_embedding: A ui-contract resource entry documenting the native Claude Code extensions data model and the planned but unimplemented UI authoring/installer operations, while stating the company exposes no actual extension-management API.
intention_for_embedding: To give a UI tool a schema-level contract for rendering and eventually bridging Claude Code's native extension surface without pretending the company already controls it.
